title: MusicGen medium/large text-to-music on Intel Arc 140V via PyTorch XPU (Windows) — first GPU music-generation numbers on this box; OpenVINO export negative result
context: BlarAI vision-trailer scoring (dev-side tooling) + tier/backend groundwork for the Cinematic Studio capability (#858). Follows the 2026-07-11 CPU baseline (musicgen_medium_cpu_2026-07-11.json).

hardware.gpu: Intel Arc 140V iGPU (Xe2, 16 GB shared LPDDR5X)

software.backend: PyTorch native Intel-GPU (XPU) device; standard transformers MusicgenForConditionalGeneration.generate(), fp32 weights, no quantization
software.isolated_venv: scratchpad musicgen/venv_xpu (separate from the CPU-baseline venv and from all BlarAI runtime venvs)

methodology: Single-run generations via gen_music.py (same script as the CPU baseline). Rates are wall-clock generate() time only; model load reported separately. seed=0, guidance_scale=3.0, do_sample=true. Full track = 7 prompted sections (11-18 s each, 97 s raw) rendered serially in one process and crossfade-concatenated (1.0 s).

medium_full_track_91s.thermal_note: Per-section rate decays 4.10 -> ~1.5 s-audio/min across the 42.6-min run — sustained-load throttling on the passively-constrained laptop iGPU; single-section jobs see the higher rate.

large_health_probe_3s.caveat: 3-s probe is overhead-dominated (first-generation kernel warmup included) — deliberately a fit/health check, not a rate figure; sustained rate comes from the full-track run.

large_full_track_91s.note: Run detached on an otherwise-quiet box; per-section rate ROSE across the run.

medium_bf16_probe_15s.vs_fp32_probe: 16% SLOWER than the identical fp32 probe (3.23); RSS essentially unchanged (9.33 vs 9.54 GB). bf16 is a loss on this path.

corrections.medium_rate_decay_attribution: The medium full-track per-section decay (4.10 -> ~1.5) was originally attributed to sustained-load thermal throttling. DISPROVEN same day: the large full-track run (detached, box otherwise quiet) shows rates RISING across a longer run (1.27 -> 2.57). The medium decay was CONTENTION from an active interactive session sharing the box, not heat.

engine_queue_finding.summary: Windows Task Manager's per-GPU 'Neural' graph is a driver submission-queue class, NOT an XMX matrix-unit meter.
engine_queue_finding.evidence: Live '\GPU Engine(*engtype_*)' counters: torch-xpu MusicGen fp32 avg 39% engtype_Compute / 0.0% neural; torch-xpu bf16 likewise compute-only; OpenVINO INT8 SDXL (BlarAI UC-010 pipeline) ran on engtype_Neural with compute at 0.0%.
engine_queue_finding.implication: OpenVINO submits through the driver's neural queue; PyTorch XPU submits through compute queues at any precision. XMX-unit utilization itself is not observable with any current tool.

negative_results.optimum_cli_openvino_export: optimum-cli export openvino --model facebook/musicgen-medium --weight-format int8 FAILS: 'musicgen ... custom or unsupported architecture' (optimum 2.2.0 / optimum-intel 2.0.0 / nncf 3.2.0 / openvino 2026.2.1). The OpenVINO route for MusicGen requires the manual notebook-style conversion (separate text-encoder/decoder/EnCodec IRs + a patched generate loop) — relevant to any future #858 OpenVINO-substrate port.
negative_results.libsndfile_vorbis_crash: soundfile/libsndfile (bundled wheel) hard-crashes the process (no traceback, exit 127) encoding this 90.6-s mono 32 kHz track to OGG/VORBIS, at both float32 and float64 input; a 2-s clip encodes fine. MP3 (MPEG_LAYER_III) encodes the same array without issue. Dev-side gotcha, tracked here as a reproducible toolchain footnote.

not_measured: INT8/INT4 quantized variants (blocked: no optimum-intel musicgen export; manual OpenVINO conversion not attempted)
co-resident Qwen3-14B contention (box was otherwise idle)
XMX matrix-unit utilization (no tool exposes it; queue-class evidence only)
perceptual quality A/B medium-vs-large (operator-judged, delivered same day, verdict pending)
